title: Personal Technical Object Catalog System (PTOCS)
description: PTOCS is the Autoregia Intelligence System (VSM System 4). It builds and maintains a structured, persistent catalog of the technical objects (software, services, hardware, references, methods, capabilities, and infrastructure) the agent relies on, enabling their discovery, retrieval, navigation, and analysis over time.
purpose: Provide a centralized index of available technical objects and their capabilities, enabling efficient discovery of the capabilities available to the agent and exposing gaps, redundancy, and dependency exposure through a statistical overlay.

retrieval_navigation: search by name, keyword, domain, capability, or free text
browse by kind, category, domain, status, or system served
capability discovery for a task or objective
retrieve descriptions, specifications, and usage information
navigate related and complementary objects via the relationship graph
explain how an object contributes to overall capabilities
statistical_overlay_analysis: coverage and composition by kind, domain, category, system served, hosting model
dependency-graph analytics (depth, fan-in/out, single points of failure)
ecosystem health (orphans, broken links, license/vendor concentration)
derivative: entry study: relation graph, capability map, dependency tree
complementary: documentation fetching and link health checks
cost and license monitoring; renewal reminders

outputs: statistical-overlay projections (coverage, gaps, redundancy, dependency, cost, freshness, health)

implementation.ui: CSS, JS, HTML (libraries permitted, frameworks not)
implementation.analysis_charts: Apache ECharts (within the Autoregia design system)
implementation.ai_integration: LLM-assisted entry extraction, classification, relation proposal, capability inference
notes: First iteration may hard-code the catalog data.
Designed to be extensible and implementation-agnostic.
Supports configurable storage, indexing backends, and ranking/retrieval strategies.
UI must conform to spec/ui.spec (warm-parchment Oxford aesthetic, shared tokens).
